events.name: Seattle Cherry Blossom & Japanese Cultural Festival
events.venue: Seattle Center (Armory, Fisher Pavilion, Exhibition Hall)
events.description: The 51st annual festival celebrating Seattle's friendship with Japan and Japan's gift of 1000 cherry trees in 1976. Features Japanese art, dance, music, martial arts, Taiko drums, contemporary Japanese arts, traditional tea ceremony, and food.

events.name: Seattle Mariners vs. Houston Astros
events.description: MLB baseball home opener series. Watch the Seattle Mariners take on the Houston Astros at T-Mobile Park.

events.name: Cameron Esposito Comedy Show
events.description: Stand-up comedy performance by Cameron Esposito with Andy Iwancio at The Crocodile.

events.name: Alingon Mitra Comedy
events.description: Stand-up comedy show featuring Alingon Mitra in Tacoma.

events.name: Moisture Festival
events.description: The world's largest comedy/variety festival presenting 30 shows featuring comedy, variety acts, and performances. Friday shows are 18+.

events.name: Les Misérables - Broadway Revival
events.description: Broadway revival of the popular musical telling an enthralling story of broken dreams and unrequited love, passion, sacrifice, and redemption.

events.name: Giselle - Pacific Northwest Ballet
events.description: Romance-period ballet performance of 'Giselle,' an ethereal, haunting, and tender masterpiece about love beyond the grave.

events.name: Community Stages Fund: Fifth Anniversary Celebration
events.description: The Seattle Symphony performs with members of the Seattle community in this free community concert.

events.name: Canto Ostinato - Classical Concert
events.venue: Emerald City Music, South Lake Union
events.description: Originally written for piano, Canto Ostinato is a big hit in the Netherlands, performed by six artists.

events.name: Washington State Spring Fair
events.venue: Washington State Fair Events Center, Puyallup
events.description: Features farm animals, pig races, performing dogs, fair food, rides, entertainers, demolition derbies, and a kids' zone.

events.name: NW Model Hobby Expo
events.description: Vendors of radio controlled cars, airplanes, boats, robots, and trains. Try it yourself and learn about clubs. Kids under 10 free.

events.name: Plate of Nations - Taste of Rainier Valley
events.description: Special deals on international meals from over 50 restaurants in Rainier Valley celebrating diverse cuisines.

events.name: Madame Butterfly - Bellevue Opera
events.description: Opera telling the story of a young Japanese woman abandoned by her American naval officer husband. Music played by Bellevue Symphony.

events.name: Belltown Art Walk
events.description: Free evening art walk between venues displaying artwork throughout Belltown.

events.name: Art BFA Showcase - Cornish College
events.description: Cornish College of the Arts showcases the work of its Bachelor of Fine Arts students. Free admission.

events.name: Grease - Village Theatre
events.description: All-American musical taking a rollicking trip back to the 1950s with the cast navigating high school and love.

events.name: Live Theater - Multiple Shows
events.venue: Various theaters (Seattle Rep, Taproot, Arts West, etc.)
events.description: Multiple theater productions including: The 39 Steps (Federal Way), Ain't Misbehavin' (Greenwood), Appropriate (Seattle Rep), Footloose (Marysville), Hurricane Diane (Green Lake), Mary Jane (Seattle Rep), Silver Lining (Edmonds), Steel Magnolias (Renton), Sweet Charity (Tacoma), Walden (Arts West).

events.name: Skagit Valley Tulip Festival
events.description: Spectacular fields of blooming tulips and daffodils. Check bloom status before visiting. Some fields require admission fee.
